meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no understanding of APIs (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing via API (Intermediate Guide)
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing strategies by automating API integrations. A high-level guide for freelancers to ship faster using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop manually calculating pricing tiers. By leveraging API-driven SaaS billing, you decouple business logic from your codebase. Use Antigravity to define your schema and Claude Code to implement the Stripe or LemonSqueezy integration. This automated workflow ensures your client's revenue engine remains scalable, secure, and ready for global deployment.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your client is breathing down your neck, and the complex web of SaaS pricing logic has turned your codebase into a brittle mess of hardcoded constants. You aren't just missing an API integration; you're missing a scalable architecture that turns a liability into a high-margin asset. 

It is time to stop treating pricing as an afterthought. By mastering API-first billing, you transform from a coder who 'fixes bugs' into a technical architect who builds revenue engines. We will use Antigravity to scaffold the infrastructure and Claude Code to weave the logic, turning hours of manual configuration into minutes of automated deployment.

steps.title: Define Pricing Schemas in Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a centralized source of truth for your pricing tiers before writing a single line of backend logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model supporting monthly/yearly tiers, feature flags, and trial periods.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create pricing_schema.json based on the Antigravity output'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Billing Service
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold a clean, decoupled billing service that consumes your schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate Node.js service that handles Stripe subscription logic using the schema generated in step 1.

steps.title: Implement Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Securely process asynchronous events from your payment provider to keep user access in sync.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Express.js middleware to verify Stripe webhook signatures and update user status in the database.

steps.title: Integrate Feature Gating
steps.description: Connect your pricing tiers to actual application features to prevent unauthorized access.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a decorator or HOC for feature gating based on the user's active subscription tier.

steps.title: Automate Deployment Testing
steps.description: Ensure your pricing logic holds up under load with automated end-to-end test suites.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Playwright test scenarios for the full subscription flow including checkout, success, and cancellation.

pro_tips: Always use idempotency keys in your API requests to prevent double-billing on network retries.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your database to prevent immediate user churn on failed payment retries.
Store pricing calculations in a separate micro-service to avoid blocking your main thread during high traffic.
Use environment-specific API keys; never hardcode test-mode credentials in production configs.
Map your database user IDs directly to the payment provider's customer metadata for seamless reporting.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing service is live, implement a analytics dashboard using the same API structures to track MRR and churn in real-time.
content.conversion_block: Don't let manual billing hold your freelance business back. Master the stack that scales. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
